audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim sacrificial integrity, idolatry adjudication, high-court authority, and king law, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for blemished sacrifice, celestial worship accusations, two-or-three-witness requirement, execution procedure, difficult-case court, contempt sanction, king selection, anti-militarization limits, anti-harem limits, anti-wealth limits, Torah copy, and royal humility.

verses.restored: You shall not sacrifice to YHWH your Divine ox or sheep in which is blemish, any bad thing, for abomination to YHWH your Divine it is.
If found in your midst, in one of your gates YHWH your Divine gives you, man or woman who does evil in eyes of YHWH your Divine by crossing his covenant,
and goes and serves other gods and bows to them, or to sun or moon or any host of heavens that I did not command,
and it is told to you and you hear, then you shall inquire diligently; and look, if truth is established, the thing certain, this abomination was done in Israel,
you shall bring out that man or that woman who did this evil thing to your gates, the man or woman, and stone them with stones so they die.
By mouth of two witnesses or three witnesses shall the one dying be put to death; by mouth of one witness he shall not be put to death.
Hand of witnesses shall be first against him to put him to death, and afterward hand of all people; so you shall remove evil from your midst.

If a matter is too difficult for you in judgment, between blood and blood, claim and claim, lesion and lesion, matters of dispute within your gates, then you shall rise and go up to place YHWH your Divine chooses.
You shall come to Levitical priests and to judge who is in those days, inquire, and they shall declare to you word of judgment.
You shall do according to word they declare to you from that place YHWH chooses, and keep to do according to all they instruct you.
According to Torah/instruction they teach you and according to judgment they say to you, you shall do; you shall not turn from word they declare to you, right or left.
The man who acts presumptuously, not listening to priest standing to serve there before YHWH your Divine or to judge, that man shall die; so you shall remove evil from Israel.
All people shall hear and fear/revere and not act presumptuously again.
When you enter land YHWH your Divine gives you and possess it and dwell in it, and say, I will set a king over me like all nations around me,
you shall surely set over yourself a king whom YHWH your Divine chooses; from midst of your brothers you shall set king over you. You may not put over you a foreigner who is not your brother.
Only he shall not multiply horses for himself or return people to Egypt to multiply horses, for YHWH said to you, You shall never return again by this way.
He shall not multiply wives for himself, lest his heart turn aside; silver and gold he shall not greatly multiply for himself.
It shall be, when he sits upon throne of his kingdom, he shall write for himself a copy of this Torah/instruction in a scroll from before Levitical priests.
It shall be with him, and he shall read in it all days of his life, so he may learn to fear/revere YHWH his Divine, to keep all words of this Torah and these statutes, to do them,
so his heart is not lifted above his brothers and he does not turn from command right or left, so he may lengthen days over his kingdom, he and his sons, in midst of Israel.

translation_notes: The blemish rule concerns sacrificial suitability and must not be transferred to disabled people as a measure of worth or holiness.
Idolatry accusations require diligent inquiry and at least two witnesses, but the execution mandate remains a religious-persecution text that must not be reenacted.
The difficult-case court centralizes legal interpretation, but verse 12's death penalty for contempt is morally dangerous and cannot authorize authoritarian religious courts.
The king law is strikingly restrictive: no military horse accumulation, no return to Egypt, no multiplying wives, and no excessive wealth.
Royal Torah study exists to prevent the king's heart from rising above his brothers.
The foreigner exclusion reflects ancient kinship politics and must not justify xenophobic citizenship or leadership bans today.
safety_note: Do not use Deuteronomy 17 to execute people for belief, stigmatize disability, create unaccountable religious courts, enforce xenophobic leadership rules, or defend monarchy as sacred domination. Center evidence, appeal, limits on military power, limits on wealth and sexual accumulation, and the principle that rulers remain siblings under Torah.
